title: Coprosma granadensis

The Rubiaceae family consists of over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species, many of which are found in tropical regions. Coprosma granadensis is native to New Zealand. It is a member of the genus Coprosma, which is a group of shrubs or small trees.
uses: Coprosma granadensis is known for its traditional medicinal uses in Polynesia. The plant's leaves and stems are used in various remedies, including treatments for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Coprosma is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the 'mirror bush' or 'mirror plant'. The health benefits of Coprosma plants are not well-documented, but the leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Coprosma plants include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coprosma plants are teas, infusions, and tinctures, although these are not widely available or standardized.
